title: 1981-04-16_Pat_147.txt
key_claims: In Dick's Hebrew-Kabbalist-Spinozist system, the representation (information) is exactly as real as the object it represents; there is no ontological degradation or "copy." The information *is* the object.

This framework solves Descartes' dualism and Parmenides' problem of empiricism: we can trust our senses because the "information" we receive maps with absolute point-for-point parallelism to the internal mind of God.
In 2-3-74, Dick apprehended the underlying "Infinity" attribute of reality, which transformed ordinary objects into a universal linguistic code.
By perceiving this attribute of Infinity, all of reality functionally becomes a sacrament, possessing self-authenticating meaning that requires no external interpretation.
recurring_concepts: **The Meta-Abstraction**: Restated here as the realization that a single "Idea" (Object) eternally instantiates in infinite places and times ("Not 2 mothers but 1 mother twice").
reading_excerpt: In Dick's Hebrew-Kabbalist-Spinozist system, the representation (information) is exactly as real as the object it represents; there is no ontological degradation or "copy." The information *is* the object.
